title: Add FeedbackAlignmentTimeline report block for alignment-over-time trends

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation: new FeedbackAlignmentTimeline backend ReportBlock + frontend renderer. Scope includes single-score and all-scores-on-scorecard modes, complete previous-period bucketing (trailing + calendar), chart visualization, registry wiring, and tests/docs updates.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented FeedbackAlignmentTimeline report block end-to-end. Backend: new class at plexus/reports/blocks/feedback_alignment_timeline.py with complete-period time buckets (trailing_1d/7d/14d/30d plus calendar_day/week/biweek/month), scorecard-only (all scores) and scorecard+score modes, per-bucket AC1 + accuracy + counts, and timezone/week_start controls. Added backend tests in feedback_alignment_timeline_test.py including bucket completeness behavior, single/all score modes, invalid bucket handling, and UUID score membership validation via section->scorecard resolution. Frontend: new renderer dashboard/components/blocks/FeedbackAlignmentTimeline.tsx with horizontal time-series chart (Recharts), series selector, compacted-output attachment loading, tooltip details, and null-gap handling. Wired block registration/exports and display labels; added docs page and storybook story. Validation: pytest plexus/reports/blocks/feedback_alignment_timeline_test.py -q => 6 passed; npx eslint on touched dashboard files => 0 errors (1 pre-existing warning in ReportTask useEffect deps).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Debugging local dashboard build failure: next/font ERR_REQUIRE_ESM from postcss requiring nanoid/non-secure. Investigating dependency graph and applying minimal lockfile/package fix to restore npm run dev on port 3001.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Resolved local dev startup failure details: dashboard node_modules was out of sync with lockfile and had nanoid 5.1.7 installed, which breaks postcss require of nanoid/non-secure and causes next/font ERR_REQUIRE_ESM. Ran clean reinstall with npm ci; now nanoid is 3.3.11 and error is gone. Verified Next serves successfully with npx next dev --webpack -p 3011 and GET / returns 200. Also found port 3001 already occupied by PID 78246 (next-server v15.5.14).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Investigating report from user that dev server starts but is not responding on port 3001. Checking listening process, child process health, and dev script behavior.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Root cause confirmed for non-responsive :3001 in dashboard dev flow: npm run dev -- -p 3001 passes -p to concurrently (not Next), and dev:web was launching plain next dev which exits under Turbopack due existing webpack config. Updated dashboard/package.json dev:web script to run next dev --webpack. Verified with PORT=3001 npm run dev and curl http://127.0.0.1:3001/ => 200.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: User still cannot reach localhost:3001 in Safari. Re-checking active listeners/processes and reproducing startup in-shell to isolate runtime vs browser issue.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Verified clean startup path in Plexus-2/dashboard: after killing stale concurrently processes, PORT=3001 npm run dev starts both WEB and DISPATCH; WEB listens on 3001 and curl to http://127.0.0.1:3001/ returns 200. Prior non-response came from stale run where only dispatcher child remained alive while web child was down.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Corrective action: reverted commit 9c5bcbcc on develop via 0e22775a so develop content is restored. Preserved feature work on separate branch feature/feedback-alignment-timeline-review at 9c5bcbcc for explicit human review before any integration.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: User requested cleanup of local commit noise. Resetting develop to origin/develop and resuming work from dedicated feature branch.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: User approved splitting branch commit into separate feature-only and dev-startup-tweak commits. Rewriting feature branch history to produce clean reviewable commits and excluding unintended tsconfig noise.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Rewrote feature branch into two clean commits: 9b3fecbf (feature-only: FeedbackAlignmentTimeline backend/frontend/docs/tests) and ab512706 (dev startup tweak: dashboard package.json dev:web uses next dev --webpack). Dropped unintended dashboard/tsconfig.json change from branch.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Ran demo report for dashboard verification using config e555be4d-8013-419f-bb63-87b348712944 with parameters {days:7, scorecard:1438}. Result: report_id 14567509-4537-4fbb-9536-dc980280558d, task_id e94a457f-139c-411a-a70b-a833c7262602, URL https://plexus.anth.us/lab/reports/14567509-4537-4fbb-9536-dc980280558d.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Created temporary report configuration 28266352-80c1-4384-963b-439f27db65e4 containing a FeedbackAlignmentTimeline block and generated report via local service path (uses local block registry): report_id 195d8e61-c2b1-4268-b21f-a56fc50307c2, task_id 7d7b7ba2-f019-4660-a491-20e3ae5a6e7d, no errors. Report includes block type FeedbackAlignmentTimeline with compacted output attachment and log attachment.
